scope_boundary: synthetic command-contract, plan-graph, and typed-job record discipline plus finite Lean predicates only; no deployed execution, planner quality, semantic-parser quality, runtime adapter behavior, artifact acceptance, source interpretation, or source-derived support-state promotion
transition_reason: The repository has command-contract, plan-graph, and typed-job schemas, protocol fixtures, finite IntentToExecution Lean predicates, exact source mappings for the Intent-to-Execution Contracts chapter, and a synthetic plan-execution contract harness. Those artifacts make contract completeness, constraint preservation, artifact traceability, dispatch receipts, and approval-gated job state executable at the record level. They still do not establish deployed runtime execution, planner quality, semantic-parser quality, tool-effect correctness, artifact acceptance, source interpretation adequacy, or model behavior.
required_artifacts: replayed intent-to-execution vertical slice with real command contracts, jobs, effects, receipts, and accepted artifacts
runtime adapter trace showing approved effects and denied effects
semantic parser or command-extraction evaluation over real tasks
artifact acceptance or verification trace over actual outputs
tool-execution replay with failure and recovery paths
independent review of contract completeness and constraint preservation
source interpretation review for intent-to-execution source mappings
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
verification_command: python3 scripts/validate_plan_execution_contracts.py && python3 scripts/validate_evidence_transitions.py && python3 scripts/validate_source_evidence_audit.py && python3 scripts/validate_v1_status_snapshot.py
negative_results: schema validation checks record shape rather than deployed execution
finite Lean predicates remain useful but too narrow for runtime execution, parser quality, tool-effect correctness, artifact acceptance, or scheduler-quality claims
source interpretation review and model behavior are not implemented
downgrade_triggers: command_contract, plan_graph, or typed_job schema drift
chapter text interpreted as deployed execution evidence
synthetic dispatch receipt interpreted as a real tool-effect receipt
finite Lean predicates treated as deployed runtime behavior
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic record discipline that passed, or after replayed intent-to-execution vertical slices, real command contracts and typed jobs, runtime adapter traces, artifact verification traces, tool-effect receipts, recovery-path replays, semantic-parser evaluation, independent contract review, and source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not prove deployed execution
schema validation does not prove semantic-parser quality
finite Lean predicates do not implement a runtime dispatcher
dispatch receipt strings do not prove tool-effect correctness
artifact refs do not prove artifact acceptance
no independent runtime, parser-quality, tool-effect, artifact-review, or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent runtime-systems, planner-quality, semantic-parser, tool-effect, artifact-acceptance, formal-methods, or source-interpretation audit
acceptance_blockers: no replayed vertical slice with real effects
no runtime adapter trace over real jobs
non_claims: does not promote Intent-to-Execution Contracts above argument
does not prove planner quality or semantic-parser quality
does not prove tool-effect correctness or artifact acceptance
